necessity|en.text: noun 1: The quality or state of being necessary, unavoidable, or absolutely requisite. (e.g., "I bought a new table out of necessity. My old one was ruined.") | noun 2: The condition of being needy; desperate need; lack | noun 3: Something necessary; a requisite; something indispensable. (e.g., "A tent is a necessity if you plan on camping.") | noun 4: Something which makes an act or an event unavoidable; an irresistible force; overruling power | noun 5: The negation of freedom in voluntary action; the subjection of all phenomena, whether material or spiritual, to inevitable causation; necessitarianism. | noun 6: Greater utilitarian good; used in justification of a criminal act. (e.g., "doctrine of necessity") | noun 7: (in the plural) Indispensable requirements (of life).

verses.text: The quality or state of being necessary, unavoidable, or absolutely requisite. (e.g., "I bought a new table out of necessity. My old one was ruined.")
The condition of being needy; desperate need; lack
Something necessary; a requisite; something indispensable. (e.g., "A tent is a necessity if you plan on camping.")
Something which makes an act or an event unavoidable; an irresistible force; overruling power
The negation of freedom in voluntary action; the subjection of all phenomena, whether material or spiritual, to inevitable causation; necessitarianism.
Greater utilitarian good; used in justification of a criminal act. (e.g., "doctrine of necessity")
